input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: StatefulSet
metadata:
  name: mongodb-replica
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  serviceName: mongodb-replica-svc
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: mongodb-replica
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: mongodb-replica
    spec:
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30
      initContainers:
      - name: init-keyfile
        image: busybox:1.36
        command:
        - sh
        - -c
        - |
          cp /etc/mongodb-keyfile/keyfile /etc/mongodb-keyfile-rw/keyfile
          chmod 400 /etc/mongodb-keyfile-rw/keyfile
          chown 999:999 /etc/mongodb-keyfile-rw/keyfile
        volumeMounts:
        - name: keyfile-secret
          mountPath: /etc/mongodb-keyfile
        - name: keyfile-rw
          mountPath: /etc/mongodb-keyfile-rw
      containers:
      - name: mongod
        image: mongo:7.0
        command:
        - mongod
        - --replSet=mongodb-replica
        - --bind_ip_all
        - --auth
        - --keyFile=/etc/mongodb-keyfile/keyfile
        ports:
        - containerPort: 27017
        volumeMounts:
        - name: data
          mountPath: /data/db
        - name: keyfile-rw
          mountPath: /etc/mongodb-keyfile
        readinessProbe:
          exec:
            command:
            - mongosh
            - --eval
            - "db.adminCommand('ping')"
          initialDelaySeconds: 10
          periodSeconds: 5
          failureThreshold: 12
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: 100m
            memory: 256Mi
          limits:
            cpu: 500m
            memory: 512Mi
      volumes:
      - name: keyfile-secret
        secret:
          secretName: mongodb-keyfile
          defaultMode: 0400
      - name: keyfile-rw
        emptyDir: {}
  volumeClaimTemplates:
  - metadata:
      name: data
    spec:
      accessModes: ["ReadWriteOnce"]
      resources:
        requests:
          storage: 1Gi
EOF

I'm realizing the core issue here is a chicken-and-egg problem with MongoDB authentication and replica set initialization. When mongod starts with auth enabled but no users exist yet, I need to initialize the replica set before creating users, but the replica set needs at least one running node first. The standard approach for MongoDB handles this by temporarily disabling auth during initialization, then enabling it once the replica set is ready. The key insight is MongoDB's localhost exception—when auth is enabled but no users exist yet, you can connect from localhost without credentials. So I can start mongod with `--auth` and `--keyFile` from the beginning, then use localhost connections to initialize the replica set and create users before any external authentication is required. The challenge is the readiness probe, which needs to be simple enough to pass before auth is fully configured—something like just checking if the port is open rather than requiring authentication.
